sections.title: First steps
sections.description: Use practical Spanish for first conversations and everyday needs.

units.title: Order at a café
units.objective: Order basic drinks and food, and ask for the bill.
units.guidebook_text: Use quiero for a direct request and quisiera for a softer request. Add por favor to be polite. Use con for “with” and sin for “without.”

reward.title: Bonus chest
reward.description: Complete the first two levels to claim this reward.

units.title: Greet people and say goodbye
units.objective: Start and end short conversations politely.
units.guidebook_text: Use buenos días in the morning, buenas tardes later in the day, and buenas noches at night. Tú is informal; usted is polite or formal.

reward.title: Bonus chest
reward.description: Complete the first two levels to claim this reward.

units.title: Introduce yourself
units.objective: Share your name, home, languages, and student or work status.
units.guidebook_text: Use me llamo to give your name and soy to describe identity or origin. Use vivo en for where you live and hablo for languages you speak.

units.title: Talk about family
units.objective: Name close family members and describe simple relationships.
units.guidebook_text: Possessive words agree with the owner, not the person described: mi madre, mi padre, mis hermanos. Use se llama to say someone’s name.

reward.description: Complete the first two levels to claim this reward.

sections.title: Everyday Spanish
sections.description: Handle common travel, shopping, routine, and food situations.

units.title: Ask for directions
units.objective: Find common places and understand short directions.
units.guidebook_text: Use ¿Dónde está…? for one place and ¿Dónde están…? for multiple places. A la derecha means “to the right,” and a la izquierda means “to the left.”

reward.title: Bonus chest
reward.description: Complete the first two levels to claim this reward.
